content.problemStatement:
Bob wanted to tell his son the password of his CodeChef account, but as there are many people present in the surroundings, Bob encodes the password. Bob‚Äôs original password consists of digits 1,2,1, 2,1,2, and 333 only.

To encode the password, Bob decided:


Instead of the digit 111, he would say ab\texttt{ab}ab.
Instead of the digit 222, he would say aba\texttt{aba}aba.
Instead of the digit 333, he would say a\texttt{a}a.
Due to noise, Bob's son did not receive the exact encoded password.

Instead, he received a string SSS where the encoded password is a subsequence of string SSS and is uniquely decodable.

Note that a string consisting of characters `a` and `b` is said to be uniquely decodable if it can be formed by encoding **exactly one** sequence of digits 1,2,1, 2,1,2, and 333.

For example, the strings `a` (formed by 333), `ab` (formed by 111), `aaab` (formed by 331331331) are uniquely decodable while `b` (cannot be formed), `aba` (can be formed by both 222 and 131313) are not uniquely decodable.

Bob's son wants to know the number of uniquely decodable subsequences in string SSS. As the answer can be very large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of one line of input which is string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of uniquely decodable subsequences of the given string modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: It is guaranteed that sum of ‚ą£S‚ą£|S|‚ą£S‚ą£ over all test cases ‚Č§2‚čÖ105\leq 2 \cdot 10^5‚Č§2‚čÖ105.

Explanation:
Test case 111: For string aa possible uniquely decodable subsequences are S1=S_1=S1‚Äč=a, S2=S_2=S2‚Äč=a, S1S2=S_1S_2=S1‚ÄčS2‚Äč=aa.
Test case 222: For string ab possible uniquely decodable subsequences are S1=S_1=S1‚Äč=a, S1S2=S_1S_2=S1‚ÄčS2‚Äč=ab.
Test case 333: For string abab possible uniquely decodable subsequences are S1=S_1=S1‚Äč=a, S3=S_3=S3‚Äč=a, S1S2=S_1S_2=S1‚ÄčS2‚Äč=ab, S1S4=S_1S_4=S1‚ÄčS4‚Äč=ab, S3S4=S_3S_4=S3‚ÄčS4‚Äč=ab, S1S3=S_1S_3=S1‚ÄčS3‚Äč=aa, S1S3S4=S_1S_3S_4=S1‚ÄčS3‚ÄčS4‚Äč=aab, S1S2S3S4=S_1S_2S_3S_4=S1‚ÄčS2‚ÄčS3‚ÄčS4‚Äč=abab.
